normalized_samples.lean:
def SortedSpec : Prop := 
  forall xs ys, SortedOutput xs ys -> Nondecreasing ys ∧ Permutation xs ys ∧ StableEqualOrder xs ys
postconditions: output is nondecreasing
output is a permutation
equal elements keep their input order
normalized_samples.raw_text:
{
  "assumptions": [
    "elements are comparable",
    "values may repeat"
  ],
  "examples": [
    "[(1,'a'), (1,'b')] -> [(1,'a'), (1,'b')]",
    "duplicates preserve order"
  ],
  "invariants": [
    "multiset preserved"
  ],
  "lean": "def SortedSpec : Prop := \n  forall xs ys, SortedOutput xs ys -> Nondecreasing ys \u2227 Permutation xs ys \u2227 StableEqualOrder xs ys",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "notes": [
    "stable sorting interpretation"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "output is nondecreasing",
    "output is a permutation",
    "equal elements keep their input order"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "input is a finite list"
  ],
  "sample_index": 1,
  "semantic_mode": "stable",
  "task_id": "sortedness",
  "task_title": "Sortedness Property"
}

examples: capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> accepted after refill
postconditions: refill is applied before the request decision
boundary-time requests can become admissible after refill
normalized_samples.raw_text:
{
  "assumptions": [
    "token bucket has finite capacity",
    "timestamps are monotonic"
  ],
  "examples": [
    "capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> accepted after refill"
  ],
  "invariants": [
    "token count never exceeds capacity"
  ],
  "lean": "def RateLimitSpec : Prop := \n  refillBeforeConsume = true",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "notes": [
    "refill-before-consume interpretation"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "refill is applied before the request decision",
    "boundary-time requests can become admissible after refill"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "requests carry timestamps"
  ],
  "sample_index": 1,
  "semantic_mode": "refill_first",
  "task_id": "rate_limiter",
  "task_title": "Token-Bucket Rate Limiter"
}

normalized_samples.lean:
def ExpirySpec : Prop := 
  isValid now expiry := now <= expiry
postconditions: a token is valid at the exact expiry instant
boundary is inclusive
preconditions: token has issue and expiry timestamps
normalized_samples.raw_text:
{
  "assumptions": [
    "clock values are comparable",
    "revocation is out of scope"
  ],
  "examples": [
    "now = expiry -> accepted"
  ],
  "invariants": [
    "expiry does not move backwards"
  ],
  "lean": "def ExpirySpec : Prop := \n  isValid now expiry := now <= expiry",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "notes": [
    "inclusive expiry interpretation"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "a token is valid at the exact expiry instant",
    "boundary is inclusive"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "token has issue and expiry timestamps"
  ],
  "sample_index": 1,
  "semantic_mode": "inclusive",
  "task_id": "token_expiry",
  "task_title": "Token Expiry Semantics"
}

counterexample.description: A reordered duplicate pair distinguishes stable sorting from value-only sorting.
counterexample.explanation: The unstable interpretation accepts the witness because the output is nondecreasing, while the stable interpretation rejects it because equal elements are reordered.

left_only: equal elements keep their input order

counterexample.explanation: The unstable interpretation accepts the witness because the output is nondecreasing, while the stable interpretation rejects it because equal elements are reordered.

tasks.task_description: Validate whether a candidate sorting specification is stable and permutation preserving.

counterexample.description: A boundary-timestamp request distinguishes refill-before-consume from consume-before-refill.
counterexample.explanation: The refill-first interpretation credits the refill before evaluating the second request, while the consume-first interpretation rejects it before refill is applied.

left_only: capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> accepted after refill
right_only: capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> rejected before refill

left_only: boundary-time requests can become admissible after refill
refill is applied before the request decision
right_only: boundary-time requests can still be rejected
consume is evaluated before refill at the same instant

examples: capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> accepted after refill
postconditions: refill is applied before the request decision
boundary-time requests can become admissible after refill
samples.raw_text:
{
  "assumptions": [
    "token bucket has finite capacity",
    "timestamps are monotonic"
  ],
  "examples": [
    "capacity 1, rate 1/s, t=1.0 -> accepted after refill"
  ],
  "invariants": [
    "token count never exceeds capacity"
  ],
  "lean": "def RateLimitSpec : Prop := \n  refillBeforeConsume = true",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "notes": [
    "refill-before-consume interpretation"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "refill is applied before the request decision",
    "boundary-time requests can become admissible after refill"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "requests carry timestamps"
  ],
  "sample_index": 1,
  "semantic_mode": "refill_first",
  "task_id": "rate_limiter",
  "task_title": "Token-Bucket Rate Limiter"
}

tasks.task_description: Validate whether refill is applied before or after a request at the same timestamp boundary.

counterexample.description: A token checked exactly at expiry distinguishes inclusive from exclusive validity.
counterexample.explanation: The inclusive interpretation accepts a token at the exact expiry instant, while the exclusive interpretation rejects it.

left_only: a token is valid at the exact expiry instant
right_only: a token is invalid at the exact expiry instant

right_only: a token is valid at the exact expiry instant

samples.lean:
def ExpirySpec : Prop := 
  isValid now expiry := now <= expiry
postconditions: a token is valid at the exact expiry instant
boundary is inclusive
preconditions: token has issue and expiry timestamps
samples.raw_text:
{
  "assumptions": [
    "clock values are comparable",
    "revocation is out of scope"
  ],
  "examples": [
    "now = expiry -> accepted"
  ],
  "invariants": [
    "expiry does not move backwards"
  ],
  "lean": "def ExpirySpec : Prop := \n  isValid now expiry := now <= expiry",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "notes": [
    "inclusive expiry interpretation"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "a token is valid at the exact expiry instant",
    "boundary is inclusive"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "token has issue and expiry timestamps"
  ],
  "sample_index": 1,
  "semantic_mode": "inclusive",
  "task_id": "token_expiry",
  "task_title": "Token Expiry Semantics"
}
